name: number-field
title: Number Field
description: A numeric input with increment/decrement steppers built on Base UI's Number Field. Supports min/max/step/largeStep, locale-aware Intl formatting (currency, percent, units), and optional scrub-on-drag. Reuses the Input box styling so it matches Input and Select. A required visible label is the accessible name; steppers are named buttons with 44px targets; state is conveyed by tokens and borders (never color alone) so it survives forced-colors mode. Field-wired, controlled and uncontrolled.
useWhen: Collecting a bounded quantity or amount where stepping by a known increment helps (quantities, ages, durations)
A currency, percent, or unit amount that benefits from locale-aware formatting
A number where min/max/step should constrain and quantize input
avoidWhen: Free-form numeric identifiers that are not arithmetic (phone, SSN, ZIP, card, confirmation codes — use Input with the right inputMode/autoComplete)
Choosing from a small fixed set of numbers (use a select or radio-group)
A value best set by dragging along a range with visible bounds (use a slider)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the input; Tab again reaches the decrease and increase buttons
ArrowUp / ArrowDown step the value by step while the input is focused
Shift+ArrowUp / Shift+ArrowDown step by largeStep; Alt+ArrowUp / Alt+ArrowDown step by smallStep
Home / End jump to min / max when those bounds are set
Enter / Space activate a focused stepper button; a stepper disabled at the min/max bound is skipped
A disabled field is removed from the tab order and cannot change value
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (each stepper is a 44x44 target; the group is min-h-11)
notes: The required visible label is the accessible name via a native htmlFor association; there is no aria-label escape hatch.

Base UI renders a text input carrying aria-roledescription='Number field' (not role=spinbutton), which lets locale formatting (currency symbols, grouping separators) live in the field while typing stays forgiving; the increment/decrement buttons carry their own accessible names.

Non-color redundancy: the group and both steppers keep visible borders in every state, and disabled/invalid are signaled by dedicated tokens plus border weight, so state survives Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode.
An optional description is linked with aria-describedby and excluded from the accessible name; repeat any format-only unit (e.g. a currency or percent suffix) there so it reaches assistive tech.
Scrub-on-drag is an optional pointer-only enhancement; the keyboard and the stepper buttons remain the primary, accessible controls so it is never the only path to a value.
The field keeps its own input id inside a Field so the Field's label does not become a second label for the same input (form-field-multiple-labels).
